patient_profile.treatment_requested: Prolonged exposure therapy beyond visit cap

denial_pattern_sources: contraindication_to_step_therapy: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; State step-therapy exception laws (NY, TX, IL)
wrong_benefit_category: CMS Essential Health Benefits Bulletin 2022; documented in Aetna member complaints
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 09/03/2026 for Prolonged exposure therapy beyond visit cap related to Post-traumatic stress disorder, chronic (F43.12). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Annual visit limit for services treating Post-traumatic stress disorder, chronic (F43.12) has been exceeded. Additional visits are not a covered benefit this plan year.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with visit limit exceeded. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

This determination applies to your employer-sponsored commercial benefit while you remain covered under an active group plan (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Post-traumatic stress disorder, chronic (F43.12). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. He has been treated under commercial coverage with Prolonged exposure therapy beyond visit cap recommended as the next appropriate step. CAPS-5 score 42; completed 12 sessions with minimal symptom change. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that visit limit exceeded bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
